req.title: Create patient (audit)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-076 mandates three verification dimensions: (1) happy-path creation of a patient with all mandatory demographics by a clinician yields HTTP 201 and a stored resource; (2) omission of any mandatory element yields HTTP 422; (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation. I derived one positive case (EP – valid partition), one negative case (NEG/DT – missing mandatory field), and one boundary case (BVA – name family field at exactly 1 character, the minimum non-empty boundary). A fourth test covers the AuditEvent end-to-end requirement (STC – state transition: request → resource created → audit record exists). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that supply an authenticated clinician token and a base URL, and cleans up created resources in teardown.

tests.name: test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior patient with the test identifier exists
actions: POST /Patient with a fully-populated FHIR Patient payload including resourceType, identifier, name (family+given), birthDate, and gender
expected: Response body contains an id field
Location header points to the new resource
trace: R-HLT-076: clinician creates patient with validated demographics → patient resource stored
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_valid_demographics_returns_201(clinician_token, base_url, cleanup_patient):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "EP-001"}],
        "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["Alice"]}],
        "birthDate": "1990-06-15",
        "gender": "female"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    patient_id = resp.json().get("id")
    cleanup_patient(patient_id)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert patient_id is not None
    assert f"/Patient/{patient_id}" in resp.headers.get("Location", "")

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /Patient with payload that omits the mandatory 'name' element
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail referencing the missing field
trace: R-HLT-076: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected → 422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_missing_name_returns_422(clinician_token, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "NEG-001"}],
        "birthDate": "1985-03-20",
        "gender": "male"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    issue_text = str(body)
    assert any(kw in issue_text.lower() for kw in ["name", "missing", "required", "mandatory"])

tests.name: test_create_patient_family_name_single_char_boundary
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Minimum valid length for family name is 1 character per server profile
actions: POST /Patient with name.family set to exactly one character ('A'), all other mandatory fields present
expected: Response body stores family name as 'A'
trace: R-HLT-076: validated demographics – BVA on minimum non-empty boundary of the family name field
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_patient_family_name_single_char_boundary(clinician_token, base_url, cleanup_patient):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "BVA-001"}],
        "name": [{"family": "A", "given": ["B"]}],
        "birthDate": "2000-01-01",
        "gender": "unknown"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    patient_id = resp.json().get("id")
    cleanup_patient(patient_id)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    stored_family = body["name"][0]["family"]
    assert stored_family == "A"
    assert patient_id is not None

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_after_patient_creation
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /Patient with valid payload to trigger creation
GET /AuditEvent filtering by entity reference to the newly created patient id
expected: At least one AuditEvent exists whose entity.what.reference contains the new patient id
AuditEvent action is 'C' (create) or equivalent server code
trace: R-HLT-076: An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_audit_event_emitted_after_patient_creation(clinician_token, base_url, cleanup_patient):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [{"system": "urn:test", "value": "STC-AUD-001"}],
        "name": [{"family": "Audit", "given": ["Test"]}],
        "birthDate": "1975-11-30",
        "gender": "male"
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Patient", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    patient_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_patient(patient_id)
    time.sleep(1)
    audit_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}", "Accept": "application/fhir+json"}
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        headers=audit_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for created patient"
    actions = [e["resource"].get("action") for e in entries if "resource" in e]
    assert any(a in ("C", "create") for a in actions)
